for sponsorship reasons, was the 14th edition of the [Copa Colombia](/wiki/Copa_Colombia "Copa Colombia"), the national cup competition for clubs of [DIMAYOR](/wiki/DIMAYOR "DIMAYOR"). It began on 10 February and ended on 17 November. The tournament comprised a total of 36 teams, and the winners were [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional"), who defeated defending champions [Junior](/wiki/Junior_F.C. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football tournament season

2016 Copa Colombia

| Copa Águila 2016 | |
| --- | --- |
| Tournament details | |
| Country | Colombia |
| Teams | 36 |
| Final positions | |
| Champions | [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional") (3rd title) |
| Runners-up | [Junior](/wiki/Junior_F.C. "Junior F.C.") |
| [2017 Copa Sudamericana](/wiki/2017_Copa_Sudamericana "2017 Copa Sudamericana") | [Deportes Tolima](/wiki/Deportes_Tolima "Deportes Tolima") |
| Tournament statistics | |
| Top goal scorer | [Miguel Borja](/wiki/Miguel_Borja "Miguel Borja") (8 goals) |
| ← [2015](/wiki/2015_Copa_Colombia "2015 Copa Colombia")  [2017](/wiki/2017_Copa_Colombia "2017 Copa Colombia") → | |

The **2016 Copa Colombia**, officially the **2016 Copa Águila** for sponsorship reasons, was the 14th edition of the [Copa Colombia](/wiki/Copa_Colombia "Copa Colombia"), the national cup competition for clubs of [DIMAYOR](/wiki/DIMAYOR "DIMAYOR"). It began on 10 February and ended on 17 November. The tournament comprised a total of 36 teams, and the winners were [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional"), who defeated defending champions [Junior](/wiki/Junior_F.C. "Junior F.C.") 3–1 on aggregate score in the final. However, neither Atlético Nacional nor Junior earned a berth to the [2017 Copa Sudamericana](/wiki/2017_Copa_Sudamericana "2017 Copa Sudamericana") for having qualified for the [Copa Libertadores](/wiki/2017_Copa_Libertadores "2017 Copa Libertadores"), with semifinalists [Deportes Tolima](/wiki/Deportes_Tolima "Deportes Tolima") qualifying instead.

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2016_Copa_Colombia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Format")]

The competition retained the format used in its most recent edition.[[1]](#cite_note-Format-1) The first stage was played by 32 teams, which were split into eight groups of four teams each on a regional basis, where teams played each other of the teams in their group twice. The 8 group winners plus the best 4 second-placed teams joined the teams qualified for the [2016 Copa Libertadores](/wiki/2016_Copa_Libertadores "2016 Copa Libertadores") ([Deportivo Cali](/wiki/Deportivo_Cali "Deportivo Cali"), [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional"), and [Santa Fe](/wiki/Independiente_Santa_Fe "Independiente Santa Fe")) and the [previous edition's](/wiki/2015_Copa_Colombia "2015 Copa Colombia") winners ([Junior](/wiki/Junior_F.C. "Junior F.C.")) in the round of 16, from where the cup continued on a home-and-away knockout basis.

## Knockout phase

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2016_Copa_Colombia&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Knockout phase")]

Each tie in the knockout phase was played in a home-and-away two-legged format. In each tie, the team which had the better overall record up to that stage hosted the second leg, except in the round of 16 where the group winners automatically hosted the second leg. In case of a tie in aggregate score, neither the away goals rule nor extra time is applied, and the tie is decided by a penalty shoot-out. [Deportivo Cali](/wiki/Deportivo_Cali "Deportivo Cali"), [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional"), [Santa Fe](/wiki/Independiente_Santa_Fe "Independiente Santa Fe"), and [Junior](/wiki/Junior_F.C. "Junior F.C.") entered the competition in the Round of 16, being joined there by the eight group winners and the four best second-placed teams.

| CONMEBOL competitions | * [Copa Libertadores](/wiki/2016_Copa_Libertadores "2016 Copa Libertadores") ([first stage](/wiki/2016_Copa_Libertadores_first_stage "2016 Copa Libertadores first stage"), [second stage](/wiki/2016_Copa_Libertadores_second_stage "2016 Copa Libertadores second stage"), [final stages](/wiki/2016_Copa_Libertadores_final_stages "2016 Copa Libertadores final stages"), [finals](/wiki/2016_Copa_Libertadores_Finals "2016 Copa Libertadores Finals")) * [Copa Sudamericana](/wiki/2016_Copa_Sudamericana "2016 Copa Sudamericana") ([elimination stages](/wiki/2016_Copa_Sudamericana_elimination_stages "2016 Copa Sudamericana elimination stages"), [final stages](/wiki/2016_Copa_Sudamericana_final_stages "2016 Copa Sudamericana final stages"), [finals](/wiki/2016_Copa_Sudamericana_Finals "2016 Copa Sudamericana Finals")) * [Recopa Sudamericana](/wiki/2016_Recopa_Sudamericana "2016 Recopa Sudamericana") * [Suruga Bank Championship](/wiki/2016_Suruga_Bank_Championship "2016 Suruga Bank Championship") * [U-20 Copa Libertadores](/wiki/2016_U-20_Copa_Libertadores "2016 U-20 Copa Libertadores") * [Copa Libertadores Femenina](/wiki/2016_Copa_Libertadores_Femenina "2016 Copa Libertadores Femenina") * [Copa Libertadores de Beach Soccer](/wiki/2016_Copa_Libertadores_de_Beach_Soccer "2016 Copa Libertadores de Beach Soccer") |
